title: Customizing Layout Template
html:
Customizing Layout Template

Add to My Topics
Add selected topic and subtopicsAdd Topic & Subtopics with topic headAdd entire publication
Watch
Download PDF
Save selected topicSave selected topic and subtopicsSave entire publication
Share
Email LinkCopy Link
Feedback
  • Last UpdatedJan 20, 2026
    • Uniface
    • 10.4

The layout template system provides a centralized mechanism for controlling the HTML structure of DSP applications. When a DSP request is processed, USYSHTTP invokes DSP.HANDLER->genResponse(), which calls the USYSHTTP_LAYOUT::GETHTMLLAYOUT global ProcScript library to generate the final HTML output.

For more information about USYSHTTP processing, see USYSHTTP.

USYSHTTP_LAYOUT::GETHTMLLAYOUT Global ProcScript Library

Purpose

Controls HTML layout generation for DSP applications.

Syntax
USYSHTTP_LAYOUT::GETHTMLLAYOUT(pIsMainDsp, pLayoutMode,
      pLayoutContent)
Parameters

The following parameters are used by the GETHTMLLAYOUT procedure:

Parameter Type Description
pIsMainDsp Boolean true if the DSP is a main DSP, false for contained DSPs
pLayoutMode String Layout mode value: "G" (Generated), "F" (Fragment), or "D" (Full Document)
pLayoutContent String Content from the DSP's Layout
Returns

A string containing the final HTML layout.

Default Implementation

The default implementation in usys.uar performs the following actions:

  • For Generated mode, returns a basic HTML structure.
  • For Fragment mode, wraps the layout content in a minimal HTML document.
  • Full Document mode, returns the layout content without modification.

Customizing the Implementation

To customize the layout template, complete the following requirements:

  • Create or import the USYSHTTP_LAYOUT library.
  • Implement or modify the GETHTMLLAYOUT global procedure.
  • Deploy the custom library to your server.

Implementation Options

You can customize the layout template using one of the following approaches.

  • Option 1: Import from Source Files
  • Option 2: Create a New Implementation

Option 1: Import from Source Files

  1. Import the source files from <install_dir>/uniface/misc/libprc.usyshttp_layout.xml.

    This creates the USYSHTTP_LAYOUT library with the default GETHTMLLAYOUT procedure implementation.

  2. Modify the implementation.

    Update the GETHTMLLAYOUT procedure as needed to customize the layout template for your application requirements.

Option 2: Create a New Implementation

  1. Create Library.
    • Library name: USYSHTTP_LAYOUT
  2. Implement Global Procedure.
    • Procedure: GETHTMLLAYOUT
    • Type: Global procedure
    • Parameters: As specified above

Deployment Steps

Perform the following steps to deploy your custom implementation:

  1. Compile the Library.

    Compile the USYSHTTP_LAYOUT library to create a UAR file.

  2. Deploy to Server.

    Add the compiled UAR file to the [RESOURCES] section of your assignment file. Ensure your custom UAR is listed before the default usys.uar so that your customizations take precedence:

    [RESOURCES]
usys.uar;other uar's

    For configuration and deployment of global ProcScript libraries, see Global ProcScript Library.

Related Links
  • Layout Mode Property

    The Layout Mode property is a component-level setting that determines how a DSP’s layout content is processed during HTML generation. It controls the interaction between custom HTML and the layout template system.

  • Layout Template System

    The layout template system provides a centralized mechanism for generating consistent initial HTML content—including <head> sections, scripts, and document structure—for DSP applications. This allows common elements to be managed in one location while individual DSPs contribute their specific content.

  • USYSHTTP

    The USYSHTTP component is a global web application request processor that handles all web requests sent to a Uniface Server via the Uniface Web Request Dispatcher (WRD).

  • UX Widgets

    Uniface UX provides a set of widgets that are implemented using the UX Widget Interface. This set is referred as UX Widgets. The sources of these widgets are available and allow changes and/or extensions to the widgets. In a default Uniface installation, you can find the sources in the <install>\uniface\webapps\uniface\common\ux folder.

  • weblayout

    Copies the layout of the dynamic server page instance and prepares to send it to the browser.

  • Global ProcScript Library

    A Global ProcScript Library contains Global ProcScript objects, which are linked into components at runtime when called from other ProcScript in the application.
